mechanism_narrative: An increase in the unemployment rate signals weakening labor market conditions that reduce corporate earnings expectations and consumer spending, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of months when UNRATE increases, or the rank correlation between UNRATE change and forward returns is positive and significant (p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.0%, restrictive monetary policy elevates borrowing costs and dampens equity risk appetite, producing negative S&P 500 forward 60-day returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of instances when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.0% over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in retail sales and rise in the unemployment rate signals a contraction in aggregate demand that amplifies recession risk beyond the effect of either series alone, driving negative S&P 500 forward 20-day returns.
failure_condition: The interaction term between RSAFS and UNRATE carries a positive coefficient (wrong sign) with t-stat > 2 in a linear model predicting 20-day returns, or the hedge portfolio sorted on this interaction produces positive average returns.

mechanism_narrative: During periods when the federal funds rate exceeds 4.0%, month-over-month increases in industrial production signal supply-driven inflation persistence that sustains tighter-for-longer monetary policy expectations, compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward 20-day returns.
failure_condition: In regimes where FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.0%, rising INDPRO is followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of observations over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in housing starts exceeding 5% signals a sharp contraction in residential investment and downstream demand for construction materials and consumer durables, leading to negative S&P 500 forward 5-day returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day returns are positive following month-over-month HOUST declines exceeding 5% in more than 50% of trigger events over a 2-year rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: The interaction between elevated federal funds rates and rising nonfarm payrolls captures the Federal Reserve's reaction function: strong job growth under restrictive policy prompts expectations of sustained tightening, producing negative S&P 500 forward 20-day returns.
regime_hint: tight labor market with restrictive policy
failure_condition: The joint effect of FEDFUNDS and PAYEMS on 20-day forward returns has a positive sign with t-stat > 2 in a linear interaction model, or the signal portfolio yields positive average returns.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth reflects the persistence of aggregate demand and corporate revenue expansion, predicting positive S&P 500 forward 60-day returns as earnings expectations adjust upward.
failure_condition: Positive GDPC1 growth is followed by negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of quarterly observations over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL rises more than 0.3% month-over-month, inflation surprises force a repricing of terminal rate expectations and increase equity risk premiums, generating negative S&P 500 forward 5-day returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day returns are positive following month-over-month CPIAUCSL increases exceeding 0.3% in more than 50% of instances over a 2-year window.
